mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and easing supply bottlenecks, which reduces input costs and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of > 1% coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 8%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 8.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal rising household confidence and future construction spending, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window or HOUST increases consistently precede negative 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals sudden supply chain disruptions or demand destruction, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following an INDPRO drop exceeding 0.8%.
